strengths: Extremely high aggregate win rate of 94.0% over a statistically significant sample of 3967 trades, confirming strong predictive power.
Highly profitable with a positive aggregate expectancy of +5.85 bps per trade.
The volatility filter successfully improved precision, increasing the win rate from its parent spec's 93.7% to 94.0%.
Performance is exceptionally consistent, with all six symbol-day backtests showing win rates between 91.3% and 95.9%, demonstrating the signal is not symbol-specific.
weaknesses: The win rate remains extraordinarily high (94.0%), which is a red flag for potential overfitting or an artifact of the idealized backtest environment, rather than a robust market edge.
The volatility filter significantly reduced signal frequency, cutting the number of trades by more than half compared to its parent spec (from >9000 to 3967), which may limit its applicability.
recommended_direction_reasoning: This spec and its parent (`iter000_obi1_gt_05`) show near-perfect predictive power (WR > 93%) at the 1-tick horizon, but this performance may be fragile. Instead of further tightening a single signal, the next step should be to improve robustness by requiring a consensus. Combining this high-WR signal with other orthogonal, high-performing signals from iteration 0 (e.g., those based on OFI or microprice) in an ensemble vote is the most logical way to validate the signal and reduce false positives.
